rulings.Case Title: THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. ALAN ARGOSINO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Determine That Narog Is A Vexatious Litigant; Req For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 10, 2012, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT FOLSOM/FIRST LLC's Motion To Determine That Narog Is A Vexatious Litigant; Request For Judicial Notice - The motion is granted. No opposition was filed and the relief sought is appropriate under Cal. Civ. Pro. §§ 391.1 and 391.3. Mr. Narog shall post bond in the amount of $12,000 no later than 9:30 a.m. April 16, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JM)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD ELMS VS. 1124 STANYAN SF, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 10, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT DAN LEE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Demurrer to the first and fifth causes of action sustained without leave to amend. No dismissal has been filed, and Plaintiff has asserted no opposition. The demurrer to the second, third, and fourth causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has ten days to amend the second, third, and fourth causes of action of the first amended complaint. The Court declines to take judicial notice of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint and Plaintiff's Opposition to Defendant Dan Lee's Motion to Vacate the Amended Default Judgment and accompanying documentation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JM)

rulings.Case Title: KYLE RICH VS. JOEL DECH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Supplemental Interrogatory And Req For Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 10, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT JOEL DECH Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Supplemental Interrogatory And Req For Monetary Sanctions; Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B as ordered by the court on April 4, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com referencing the case name and number in the subject line, with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hek

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. M & R WINE AND SPIRITS, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 10, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted as to defendants M&R Wine and Spirits, Inc. and Major S. Bains, individually as personal guarantor of M&R Wine and Spirits, Inc., in total requested amount of $10,389.41, less $900 for estimated 3 hours not spent on reply or hearing attendance, for total of $9,489.41. The Applications are granted based on the evidence presented by plaintiff/applicant. Defendants have not filed any opposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com referencing the case name and number in the subject line, with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: ERNICE FAGAN VS. SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 10, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANT ALLIED MEDICAL SERVICES DBA KING AMERICAN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer the First Amended Complaint is sustained. Plaintiff is given ten days leave to amend the complaint to: (1) specify a cause of action and (2) allege, if she can in good faith, (a) facts sufficient to support the cause of action and (b) facts sufficient to support the cause of action against Defendant Allied Medical Services as a corporate employer. rulings.Case Title: KENNETH BROOKS et al VS. SUTTER HEALTH INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 10, 2012, Line 15, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS, DBA CALIFORNIA, SUTTER HEALTH DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Sutter Health's Demurrer to the first and second cause of action overruled. Sutter Health's and California Pacific Medical Center's demurrers to the third cause of action for conversion sustained without leave to amend. The Labor Code displaces a conversion claim on these facts. (Thomas v. Home Depot USA Inc. (2007) 527 F.Supp.2d 1003, 1009.) Defendants have ten days to answer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JM)
